sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section II, List of Answers on Topics 43:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section II, List of Answers on Topics 43:1
items.html: Each and every Sefira is considered to have a Rosh, called GAR, and a Guf, called ZAT (See answer 64). The Sefira Keter has GAR and ZAT. Likewise, Hochma has GAR and ZAT, and so does Bina, and so on. The position of each ten Sefirot GAR and ZAT of circles is only in the place of the Rosh and GAR of straightness since the ten Sefirot of circles of the Sefira Keter revolve only around the GAR of Keter of straightness, and ZAT of Keter of straightness are vacant of circles (see Item 89). Also, every ten Sefirot of Hochma of circles revolve solely around the GAR of Hochma of straightness, and ZAT of Hochma of straightness are vacant of circles, and so are all of them. (Part Two, Chapter One, Inner Light, Item 90)
